calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-11-2012 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jun-11-2012 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Ellen Chaitin, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Sonia Wong #11288.

Elizabeth MacDonald, Esq. special appearance for the Plaintiff: S.J. Distributors, Inc.
Plaintiff's Witness Ying Yeung was placed under oath and direct examined.
No appearance by Defendant Din w. Chan dba Mandarin Villa Restaurant.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff and against defendant for Judgment as follows:
Damages $29517.30
Prejudgemtn interest $3226.69
Costs $545.00
Attoreney's Fees $1000.00, for a total of $34,288.99
Within 2 days, Counsel to prepare the Judgment and submit to dept. 514 for signature.

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-19-2012 Set For Trial On Jun-11-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For An Order Establishing Truth Of Facts Specified In Request For Admissions As To Din W Chan Dba Mandarin Villa Restaurant; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF S.J. DISTRIBUTORS, INC'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER ESTABLISHING TRUTH OF FACTS SPECIFIED IN REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS AS TO DIN W CHAN DBA MANDARIN VILLA RESTAURANT; AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.
Appearances: Thomas A. E. Hesketh (756.0336) specially appearing for plaintiff and moving party; no other appearances.
There being no other appearance, defendant is deemed to have stipulated to have this matter decided by a Judge Pro Tem. Mr. Hesketh signed a stipulation to that effect on behalf of the plaintiff. Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Denied, without prejudice. No opposition was filed, but the moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The court will prepare a form of order. (302/JPT) Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin; Not reported
